data.description: If the DM determines that the adventurers encounter creatures while they're traveling, it's up to both groups to decide what happens. Either might take action or wait and see.

A fall from great height is one of the common hazards an adventurer faces. At the end of a fall, an creature takes ld6 bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it fell, to a maximum of 20d6. The creature lands prone and takes damage unless it avoids falling.

The presence or absence of light in an environment creates categories of illumination: bright, dim, and darkness. Bright light allows creatures to see normally. Even the gloomiest light provides some brightness, as do torches, lanterns, fires, and other sources of illumination within a specified radius.
